metadata.title:
Ora VES' ULTIMATUM ON


NEW EAST OLIVE BRIDGE

pages.raw_text:
citys bridge at East Olive avenue.
When the Washington Water
Power Co. applied for a franchise
to cross the Howard street bridge
the war for territory between the
Traction company's lines and the
Washington Water Power lines
was bitter. The use of the Howard
street bridge enabled them to in
wade practically the only good ter
ritory we had where their lines
were not already constructed and
in competition to ours.


AS a conditio INTO receiving the
franchise the Washington Water
Power Co. was required to put the
bridges In good condition for car.
frying their traffic as we'll as the
other traffic passing over them.
EShortly after this was done one of
these bridges fell because of high
water or some unexplained reason.
lin agreeing as to the replacement
of this bridge certain obligations
were entered into, perhaps not le-
gal. but at least moral, on the
part of the Water Power company
with respect to bearing the expense
of putting all the bridges in good
condition, and from this grew the
offer to contribute one third to the
cost of building new bridges on
Howard street.


AS to the Monroe street bridge,
we have only to point out to you
that there is existing between the
Washington Water Power Co. and
the city of Spokane an alleged con-
tract which purportetd to require
the Washington Water Power Co.
to bear one half the expense of
repairs on the Monroe street
bridge or any other bridge that
might be put in its place.


In endeavoring to settle the con-
troversy that arose in the matter,
which involved large sum of
money, the Washington Water
Power Co. as we understand, offer
ed as a compromise to pay very
considerable sum of money, $780,
900, toward a new bridge, but the
conditions under which it made
this offer related to it's purported
legal obligations, whatever they
may be. and not as to the value
of the bridge for use by street
railway per Sc.


We beg to say in conclusion that
so far as our financial obligations
are concerned we would much rath-
81 that the city reject than accept
the offer made. 1t would not have
lSeen made had 1t not been at the
solicitation of the representative of
a constituency east of the river.
which We serve. with which we
have always been on good terms
and with which we hope to con.
tinue on good terms.


We hake this offer not because
OF any legal obligation we deem
ed imposed on us, but as part or
the plan we have in the past en-
deavored to carry out and will in
the future hope to follow. Of meet
ing, in SO far as it is possible for
us to do In a businesslike conduct
of our affairs. with the wishes of
the people OF this city.


If your honorable body in its
wisdom sees fit to decline this offer
1t can only mean that We shall have
to abide by the conditions as they
now exist, and We trust that what
has occurred shall not alter our re-
lations to the people of the city.
Spokane & Inland Empire Railroad
Co. by Jay p. Graves. President.
